journal_entry.rationale: Inventory loss due to natural disaster is recognized as an expense (loss). The debit to Loss on Inventory (or similar expense account) reflects the economic loss, while the credit to Inventory reduces the asset account by the carrying amount of the destroyed goods. Under IFRS, inventory write-offs due to casualty are expensed in the period incurred. No tax implications as this is a loss event, not a taxable supply or purchase. The tuples correctly identify this as expense increase (debit) and asset decrease (credit).

output.reason: Inventory destroyed = asset decrease. The inventory account must be credited to remove the destroyed goods from the books. The loss will be debited to an expense or loss account.

credit_classifier.reason: Inventory destroyed = asset decrease. The inventory account must be credited to remove the destroyed goods from the books. The loss will be debited to an expense or loss account.

output.reason: Inventory destroyed by typhoon is an abnormal loss/casualty. The debit records the expense increase (loss on inventory destruction). The inventory asset itself will be credited (decreased) on the credit side.

output.reason: Entry correctly records casualty loss from typhoon. Debit to Loss on Inventory (expense increase) $2,000 and credit to Inventory (asset decrease) $2,000. Entry complies with IFRS treatment of inventory write-offs due to natural disaster.
